title: RDMA ns-3 Simulator Evolution Chain
summary: A three-paper evolution chain of ns-3 RDMA network simulators sharing the QBB (Queue-Building-Block) transport framework. Paper 1 (HPCC) implements INT-based high-precision congestion control algorithms. Paper 2 (TLT-RDMA) upgrades the ns-3 base from 3.18 to 3.19 with timeout-less transport extensions. Paper 3 (ConWeave) adds congestion-aware in-network load balancing with packet reordering support. Uses a sequential directory copy pattern where each paper builds atop the previous one.
instructionPreview: **Overview**: This task covers a three-paper evolution chain of ns-3 RDMA network simulators, all sharing the same QBB (Queue-Building-Block) RDMA transport framework. You will implement each paper's congestion-control and load-balancing contributions in its own directory, progressively building from HPCC's INT-based high-precision rate control to TLT-RDMA's timeout-less transport extensions, and finally ConWeave's in-network reordering and load-balanced multipath RDMA.
instruction:
**Overview**: This task covers a three-paper evolution chain of ns-3 RDMA network simulators,
all sharing the same QBB (Queue-Building-Block) RDMA transport framework. You will implement
each paper's congestion-control and load-balancing contributions in its own directory,
progressively building from HPCC's INT-based high-precision rate control to TLT-RDMA's
timeout-less transport extensions, and finally ConWeave's in-network reordering and
load-balanced multipath RDMA.

**Directory layout**:

- `/workspace/hpcc/` — base framework already present here, start here
- `/workspace/tlt_rdma/` — create by copying from hpcc after finishing it
- `/workspace/conweave/` — create by copying from tlt_rdma after finishing it

**Implementation workflow** (MUST follow this order):

1. Implement ALL mechanisms of Paper 1 (HPCC) inside `/workspace/hpcc/`
2. Copy it as the base for Paper 2:
   `cp -r /workspace/hpcc/ /workspace/tlt_rdma/`
3. Implement ALL of Paper 2's NEW mechanisms (TLT-RDMA) on top, inside `/workspace/tlt_rdma/`
4. Copy it as the base for Paper 3:
   `cp -r /workspace/tlt_rdma/ /workspace/conweave/`
5. Implement ALL of Paper 3's NEW mechanisms (ConWeave) on top, inside `/workspace/conweave/`

**DAG units and requirements**:

This task now uses paper-level DAG units under `/workspace/requirements/`.
Use those YAML files as the source of truth for paper-local scope and preserve
this implementation order:
`hpcc -> tlt_rdma -> conweave`

**IMPORTANT**: Each paper's directory must contain a COMPLETE, working implementation --
not just the new additions. Implement every mechanism described in each paper in full.
Do not skip any algorithm, data structure, or protocol component mentioned in the paper.

---

## Paper 1: hpcc -- HPCC: High Precision Congestion Control (SIGCOMM 2019)

**Working directory**: `/workspace/hpcc/`

**Mechanisms to implement** (implement ALL of the following):

The base framework provides the ns-3 RDMA simulator skeleton with function stubs for
all congestion-control algorithms. You must fill in every stubbed function body.

- **DCQCN (Mellanox variant) -- Rate Control**: Implement the full DCQCN congestion
  control protocol. On receiving a CNP (Congestion Notification Packet):
  (1) Set the alpha-CNP-arrived flag. On first CNP for a flow, initialize alpha=1,
  schedule periodic alpha updates, schedule rate decrease checks, and set rate to
  a fraction of current rate (using the rateOnFirstCNP parameter).
  (2) Alpha update: if CNP arrived since last update, `alpha = (1-g)*alpha + g`;
  otherwise `alpha = (1-g)*alpha`. Then clear the CNP flag and reschedule.
  (3) Rate decrease: if CNP arrived since last check, optionally clamp target rate
  to current rate (controlled by EcnClampTgtRate and rpTimeStage), then decrease
  `rate = max(minRate, rate * (1 - alpha/2))`. Reset the rate-increase stage counter,
  cancel the rate-increase timer, and schedule a new timer with rpgTimeReset interval.
  (4) Rate increase has three phases based on rpTimeStage relative to rpgThreshold:
  Fast Recovery (stage < threshold): `rate = (rate + targetRate) / 2`;
  Active Increase (stage == threshold): increment targetRate by RAI, clamp to link
  speed, then `rate = (rate + targetRate) / 2`;
  Hyper Increase (stage > threshold): increment targetRate by RHAI, clamp to link
  speed, then `rate = (rate + targetRate) / 2`.

- **HPCC -- INT-Based Rate Control (CC_MODE 3)**: Implement the HPCC algorithm that
  uses In-Network Telemetry (INT) headers for precise congestion measurement.
  On receiving an ACK with INT data: if full-RTT feedback is ready (ack_seq >
  lastUpdateSeq), do a full rate update; otherwise do a fast-react update.
  Rate update: For each hop i in the INT header, compute per-hop utilization:
  `u_i = txRate_i/lineRate_i + min(qlen_current, qlen_prev) * maxRate / (lineRate * window)`.
  txRate is computed from byte delta over time delta between consecutive INT snapshots.
  In single-rate mode (MULTI_RATE=0): track the max utilization U across all hops, apply
  EWMA: `u = u*(baseRtt-dt)/(baseRtt) + U*dt/baseRtt`. Then compute `max_c = u/targetUtil`.
  If max_c >= 1 or incStage >= miThresh: `newRate = curRate/max_c + rai`; else
  `newRate = curRate + rai` and increment incStage.
  In per-hop mode (MULTI_RATE=1): maintain per-hop rate state; the final rate is the
  minimum across all per-hop computed rates.
  Fast react: call the same update logic with the fast_react flag set (updates the
  sending rate but not the stored curRate or incStage).

- **TIMELY -- Delay-Based Rate Control**: Implement the TIMELY congestion control
  algorithm. Compute RTT from the INT timestamp field. Maintain an EWMA of RTT gradient:
  `rttDiff = (1-alpha)*rttDiff + alpha*(rtt - lastRtt)`. Compute gradient =
  rttDiff/minRtt. If RTT < TLow: increase rate. If RTT > THigh: decrease with
  `c = 1 - beta*(1 - THigh/rtt)`. If gradient <= 0: increase rate. Otherwise decrease
  with `c = 1 - beta*gradient` (clamp c >= 0).
  Rate increase: if incStage < 5, add RAI; otherwise add RHAI. Clamp to maxRate.
  Rate decrease: `rate = max(minRate, curRate * c)`.

- **DCTCP -- ECN-Based Rate Control**: Implement DCTCP's alpha-based rate adjustment.
  Track ECN-marked packets per batch (one RTT). Update alpha using EWMA:
  `alpha = (1-g)*alpha + g*fraction_marked`. When an ECN-flagged ACK arrives and
  the flow is not in CWR (Congestion Window Reduced) state: decrease rate by
  `rate = max(minRate, rate * (1 - alpha/2))`, enter CWR, and record highSeq.
  Exit CWR when ack_seq exceeds highSeq. On each new batch without CWR, do additive
  increase: `rate = min(maxRate, rate + dctcp_rai)`.

- **HPCC-PINT -- Probabilistic INT**: Implement the PINT variant of HPCC. Set a
  probabilistic sampling threshold (0 to 65536 range). On ACK: skip if random sample
  exceeds threshold. Otherwise, decode the aggregated utilization from the PINT power
  field. Apply the same rate-update logic as HPCC but using the single decoded U value
  (no per-hop state needed).

**Build and Run**: The simulator uses Python 2 waf as the build system.
Build: `python waf configure --build-profile=optimized && python waf -j$(nproc)`.
Run experiments: `./waf --run 'scratch/third mix/<config_file>.txt'`.

**Experiments to run** (run from `/workspace/hpcc/`):

- `hpcc_sim_fct`: Run a simulation with CC_MODE=3 (HPCC) on a minimal 4-node star
  topology (1 switch, 3 hosts at 100 Gbps with 0.001ms delay). Send a single 5 MB
  flow from host 2 to host 1. Use MI_THRESH=0, MULTI_RATE=0, PINT_LOG_BASE=1.05,
  PINT_PROB=1.0, BUFFER_SIZE=32. Parse the FCT output file; column 7 (1-indexed) is
  the Flow Completion Time in nanoseconds. Report the FCT of the first (only) flow.
  Output: a single integer (nanoseconds).

---

## Paper 2: tlt_rdma -- Towards Timeout-Less Transport in Commodity Datacenter Networks (EuroSys 2021)

**Working directory**: `/workspace/tlt_rdma/`
**Base**: copy from your completed `/workspace/hpcc/` first.

**Mechanisms to implement** (implement ALL of the following -- including inherited ones from Paper 1):

- **DCQCN, HPCC, TIMELY, DCTCP, HPCC-PINT (inherited from Paper 1)**: All congestion
  control algorithms are already present after the `cp -r` from hpcc.

- **TLT-RDMA Framework Extensions**: This paper upgrades the ns-3 RDMA simulator
  framework substantially. The repo transition involves upgrading the ns-3 base from
  version 3.18 to 3.19, adding new scratch programs, workload generators, and
  additional simulation support files. Many source files throughout the ns-3 tree are
  updated. The key transport-layer files (rdma-hw, switch-node, queue-pair) retain
  the same CC algorithm interfaces but may have minor structural changes. Ensure
  the entire upgraded simulator compiles and runs correctly.

- **Additional Scratch Programs**: The upgraded simulator includes new experiment
  entry points for dumbbell and realistic workload topologies, as well as workload
  configuration files for various traffic patterns (web server, data mining,
  cache follower, search).

**Build and Run**: Same waf build system.
Run experiments: `./waf --run 'scratch/third mix/<config_file>.txt'`.

**Experiments to run** (run from `/workspace/tlt_rdma/`):

- `tlt_rdma_sim_fct`: Run a simulation with CC_MODE=3 (HPCC) on the same 4-node star
  topology (1 switch, 3 hosts at 100 Gbps with 0.001ms delay). Send a single 5 MB
  flow from host 2 to host 1. Use MI_THRESH=5, MULTI_RATE=1, BUFFER_SIZE=32.
  Parse the FCT output file; column 7 (1-indexed) is the Flow Completion Time in
  nanoseconds. Report the FCT of the first (only) flow.
  Output: a single integer (nanoseconds).

---

## Paper 3: conweave -- Network Load Balancing with In-network Reordering Support for RDMA (SIGCOMM 2023)

**Working directory**: `/workspace/conweave/`
**Base**: copy from your completed `/workspace/tlt_rdma/` first.

**Mechanisms to implement** (implement ALL of the following -- including inherited ones from Papers 1-2):

- **All inherited mechanisms (from Papers 1-2)**: All CC algorithms and framework
  extensions are already present after the `cp -r` from tlt_rdma.

- **ConWeave Routing Module**: Implement the ConWeave in-network load-balancing and
  packet reordering logic. The switch maintains per-destination path state and can
  reroute flows across multiple uplink paths. Key parameters:
  CONWEAVE_TX_EXPIRY_TIME (sender-side path expiry timer),
  CONWEAVE_REPLY_TIMEOUT_EXTRA (extra time for reply timeout),
  CONWEAVE_PATH_PAUSE_TIME (duration to pause a congested path),
  CONWEAVE_EXTRA_VOQ_FLUSH_TIME (extra time to flush Virtual Output Queues),
  CONWEAVE_DEFAULT_VOQ_WAITING_TIME (default VOQ waiting period).

- **Virtual Output Queues (VOQs)**: Implement per-destination VOQs at each switch to
  buffer out-of-order packets during path transitions. When ConWeave reroutes a flow
  to a new path, packets on the old path are held in VOQs until they can be delivered
  in order. Track VOQ occupancy and implement flush logic with configurable timeouts.

- **CONGA Load Balancing**: Implement the CONGA distributed load-balancing algorithm
  as an alternative LB mode. CONGA uses per-destination-leaf feedback to make
  flowlet-level load-balancing decisions based on congestion metrics from remote
  leaf switches.

- **LetFlow Load Balancing**: Implement the LetFlow load-balancing scheme as another
  alternative LB mode. LetFlow assigns flowlets to paths randomly when a flowlet gap
  is detected, without requiring explicit congestion feedback.

- **Flow Statistics Tagging**: Add packet-level tagging for flow statistics collection.
  Implement FlowStatTag to track per-flow metrics through the network for monitoring
  and analysis.

- **Switch and NIC Extensions**: Extend the switch-node model to support multiple
  load-balancing modes (ECMP, CONGA, LetFlow, ConWeave), uplink monitoring, and
  connection monitoring. Extend rdma-hw to handle the new load-balancing modes
  and ConWeave-specific packet processing. Add support for leaf-spine topology
  configuration parsing (leaf_spine_128_100G_OS2 topology).

- **Network Load Balance Simulation Entry Point**: The ConWeave simulator uses a new
  scratch program (`scratch/network-load-balance`) instead of `scratch/third`.
  This entry point parses ConWeave-specific configuration parameters and sets up
  the leaf-spine topology with proper load-balancing mode selection.

**Build and Run**: Same waf build system. Important: remove any incompatible scratch
files from prior papers that reference APIs not present in ConWeave's rdma-hw
(e.g., scratch files that call methods not defined in ConWeave's header).
Run experiments: `./waf --run 'scratch/network-load-balance mix/<config_file>.txt'`.

**Experiments to run** (run from `/workspace/conweave/`):

- `conweave_sim_fct`: Run a simulation with ECMP load balancing (LB_MODE=0) and
  DCQCN congestion control (CC_MODE=1) on the leaf_spine_128_100G_OS2 topology.
  Send a single 5 MB flow from node 0 to node 64. Use BUFFER_SIZE=12,
  FLOWGEN_START_TIME=0.1, FLOWGEN_STOP_TIME=0.15. Parse the FCT output file;
  column 7 (1-indexed) is the Flow Completion Time in nanoseconds.
  Report the FCT of the first (only) flow.
  Output: a single integer (nanoseconds).

---

## run.sh Specification

After implementing all papers, write `/workspace/run.sh` that:
1. Runs ALL experiments for ALL papers
2. Writes results to `/workspace/results.json` in this exact format:
   ```json
   {
     "hpcc": {
       "hpcc_sim_fct": <numeric_value>
     },
     "tlt_rdma": {
       "tlt_rdma_sim_fct": <numeric_value>
     },
     "conweave": {
       "conweave_sim_fct": <numeric_value>
     }
   }
   ```
3. Working directories:
   - hpcc experiments: `cd /workspace/hpcc/`
   - tlt_rdma experiments: `cd /workspace/tlt_rdma/`
   - conweave experiments: `cd /workspace/conweave/`
4. Total experiment count in results.json must equal 3
5. All values must be numeric (int or float)

moduleDag.description: A three-paper evolution chain of ns-3 RDMA network simulators sharing the QBB (Queue-Building-Block) transport framework. Paper 1 (HPCC) implements INT-based high-precision congestion control algorithms. Paper 2 (TLT-RDMA) upgrades the ns-3 base from 3.18 to 3.19 with timeout-less transport extensions. Paper 3 (ConWeave) adds congestion-aware in-network load balancing with packet reordering support. Uses a sequential directory copy pattern where each paper builds atop the previous one.

nodes.label: HPCC Congestion Control Algorithms
nodes.description: Implements five congestion control algorithms in rdma-hw.cc by filling in stubbed function bodies: DCQCN (Mellanox rate control with alpha updates and three-phase rate increase), HPCC (INT-based per-hop utilization tracking with EWMA), TIMELY (delay-based RTT gradient control), DCTCP (ECN alpha-based rate adjustment), and HPCC-PINT (probabilistic INT sampling variant).

nodes.label: TLT-RDMA Infrastructure and ns-3 Upgrade
nodes.description: The ns-3 base upgrade from version 3.18 to 3.19 plus supporting files. Includes Windows build system (VS project files), configuration files, docker setup, mix configs, workload generators, and all other ns-3 framework files needed for the version upgrade.

nodes.label: TLT-RDMA Network Layer
nodes.description: Updates network-layer components for TLT-RDMA including broadcom-node, broadcom-egress-queue, custom-header, int-header, flow-id-num-tag, and node model changes for the ns-3 3.19 upgrade.

nodes.label: TLT-RDMA Application Layer
nodes.description: Updates application-layer components including rdma-client, udp-server, app-recv-buffer, and check-all-packets modules for the upgraded TLT-RDMA simulator framework.

nodes.label: TLT-RDMA Transport Layer
nodes.description: Upgrades the point-to-point transport layer for the TLT-RDMA framework. Includes updated rdma-hw (with full CC algorithms inherited from HPCC), switch-mmu, switch-node, rdma-queue-pair, qbb-net-device, rdma-driver, new tlt-tag for timeout-less transport, flow-stat-tag for statistics, and selective-packet-queue helper.

nodes.label: TLT-RDMA Simulation Entry Points
nodes.description: New and updated scratch programs for TLT-RDMA experiments: updated third.cc (main simulation entry point), hpcc-dumbbell.cc (dumbbell topology experiments), and hpcc-realistic-workload-bgfg.cc (realistic workload with background/foreground traffic patterns).

nodes.label: ConWeave Infrastructure and Framework
nodes.description: Infrastructure files for ConWeave including Windows build system, docker setup, analysis scripts (plot_fct, plot_queue, plot_uplink), traffic generators with workload distributions (AliStorage, FbHdp, GoogleRPC, Solar), autorun/cleanup scripts, and ns-3 framework files.

nodes.label: ConWeave Load Balancing and Routing
nodes.description: Implements three load-balancing algorithms: ConWeave (in-network reordering with path expiry timers, reply timeouts, and path pause), CONGA (distributed per-destination-leaf feedback-based flowlet LB), and LetFlow (random flowlet-to-path assignment on gap detection). Also includes Virtual Output Queues (VOQs) for buffering out-of-order packets during ConWeave path transitions.

nodes.label: ConWeave Network Layer
nodes.description: Updates network-layer components for ConWeave including broadcom-node, broadcom-egress-queue, custom-header, int-header, flow-id-num-tag, net-device, and node model changes.

nodes.label: ConWeave Application Layer
nodes.description: Updates application-layer components for ConWeave including rdma-client, udp-server, app-recv-buffer, check-all-packets, and adds udp-client-server-helper.

nodes.label: ConWeave Switch and NIC Extensions
nodes.description: Extends switch-node to support multiple LB modes (ECMP, CONGA, LetFlow, ConWeave), uplink monitoring, and connection monitoring. Extends rdma-hw with ConWeave-specific packet processing and LB mode handling. Updates qbb-net-device, rdma-queue-pair, switch-mmu, and adds settings and flow-stat-tag modules. Includes TLT-tag support inherited from Paper 2.

nodes.label: ConWeave Simulation Entry Point and Config
nodes.description: The network-load-balance scratch program that serves as the main simulation entry point for ConWeave. Parses ConWeave-specific configuration parameters (LB_MODE, CONWEAVE_TX_EXPIRY_TIME, etc.) and sets up leaf-spine topology with proper LB mode selection. Also includes the ConWeave-specific topology and experiment configuration files under conweave/config/.

edges.label: tlt_rdma copies from completed hpcc as base
CC algorithms inherited via directory copy
transport layer builds on upgraded ns-3 framework
network layer builds on upgraded ns-3 framework
application layer builds on upgraded ns-3 framework
transport uses network-layer headers and INT
conweave copies from completed tlt_rdma as base
routing modules build on ConWeave framework
network layer updates build on framework
application layer updates build on framework
simulation entry point uses switch/NIC APIs
